tree.setup: You're showing a Chinese friend photos on your phone and they ask about your family.
tree.setup_zh: 你在给中国朋友看手机上的照片，他们问起你的家人。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这张照片上的人是谁啊？
turns.text_pinyin: zhè zhāng zhàopiàn shàng de rén shì shéi a?
turns.text_en: Who are the people in this photo?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell them about your family members in the photo.

options.text_zh: 这是我爸爸妈妈，还有我姐姐。
options.text_pinyin: zhè shì wǒ bàba māma, háiyǒu wǒ jiějie.
options.text_en: These are my mom and dad, and my older sister.
options.feedback: Natural and complete — identifies each person clearly.

options.text_zh: 这是我的家人，我们家有四个人。
options.text_pinyin: zhè shì wǒ de jiārén, wǒmen jiā yǒu sì ge rén.
options.text_en: This is my family, there are four people in our family.
options.feedback: Gives the overview but doesn't identify who's who — they're looking at specific people.

options.text_zh: 左边是我爸爸，右边是妈妈。
options.text_pinyin: zuǒbiān shì wǒ bàba, yòubiān shì māma.
options.text_en: On the left is my dad, on the right is my mom.
options.feedback: Good spatial detail but only mentions two people — what about the rest?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你姐姐看起来很年轻！她做什么工作？
turns.text_pinyin: nǐ jiějie kàn qǐlai hěn niánqīng! tā zuò shénme gōngzuò?
turns.text_en: Your sister looks very young! What does she do for work?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell them about your sister's job.

options.text_zh: 她是老师，在一个小学教书。
options.text_pinyin: tā shì lǎoshī, zài yī ge xiǎoxué jiāoshū.
options.text_en: She's a teacher, she teaches at a primary school.
options.feedback: Specific and conversational — gives both the job and the workplace.

options.text_zh: 她在北京工作，是个医生。
options.text_pinyin: tā zài běijīng gōngzuò, shì ge yīshēng.
options.text_en: She works in Beijing, she's a doctor.
options.feedback: Informative — mentions location and profession. Solid answer.

options.text_zh: 我姐姐现在还在上大学呢。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ jiějie xiànzài hái zài shàng dàxué ne.
options.text_en: My sister is still in university.
options.feedback: Doesn't directly answer about work, but explains why — the 呢 softens it naturally.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese culture, asking about family members' ages, jobs, and marital status is common and not considered intrusive. Saying someone 看起来很年轻 (looks young) is always a compliment. Chinese kinship terms are very specific — 姐姐 (older sister) and 妹妹 (younger sister) are different words, unlike English.
